etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 从(從) (cóng, "follow") -> follow; from
etymology.interpretation: 從(從) (cóng, "follow") uses the movement component, 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk"), to signify the concept of walking or following in a direction. Together with 从(從) as the phonetic element providing the sound, this combination effectively represents the idea of following someone or something on a path, hence the meanings 'follow' and 'from'.
etymology.simplification: This character retains its original traditional form from Chinese history, whereas its simplified counterpart 从 suggests a person walking with another person, highlighting a reduction of strokes for simplicity.

examples.chinese: 我从家里出发(我從家裡出發)，去学校(去學校)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng jiā lǐ chūfā, qù xuéxiào.
examples.english: I set off from home to go to school.

examples.chinese: 他从不迟到(他從不遲到)，总是准时来(總是準時來)。
examples.pinyin: Tā cóng bù chídào, zǒng shì zhǔnshí lái.
examples.english: He is never late and always arrives on time.

examples.chinese: 从今天开始(從今天開始)，我要努力学习(我要努力學習)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng jīntiān kāishǐ, wǒ yào nǔlì xuéxí.
examples.english: Starting from today, I will study hard.
